audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in buffer.cc are safe at runtime: WriteBuffer._buf_list blocks are only added via Extend() which null-checks TranBufPool::malloc() before push; all BufHandle.data values passed to ReadBuffer::Append() originate from null-checked pool allocations (verified at rpc_message_stream.h:695-706 and stream line 459); all SwapOut() callers pass ReadBufferPtr(new ReadBuffer()).get() which cannot be null. The missing defensive SCHECK on SwapOut's `is` parameter is a style inconsistency, not an exploitable gap.
